text:
Ulster Gazette - News - JJ’s star power wows Hollywood
August 16, 2016        Read all here.....
  
Keady’s JJ McParland in LA where she has met up with Hollywood movie studio 
bosses.BRITAIN'S Got Talent star Jesse Jane McParland is on the verge of 
becoming a star of the big screen. 

Since the 10 year-old burst unto our screens in the last series of BGT millions of people around the world have witnessed her extraordinary talent.
 And now studio bosses in LA are desperate to get JJ signed up to star in the movies.
The Keady girl, along with her dad Gary enjoyed an all-expenses-paid trip to La La Land last week to meet with a major Holywood studio. The pair were joined on the trip by JJ's management team of Brooke Kinsella and Brian Gersh and while they are keeping tight-lipped for now it's understood major developments are in 
the pipeline very soon.
It's been a whirlwind time for Jesse Jane since her Britain's Got Talent appearances. 
 
Millions of people around the world have viewed the online videos of her competitions, she has been in constant demand for public appearances and has even appeared on the Ellen Show. On top of this she has received numerous offers from major advertising agencies to front national campaigns for a host of different products.
 
But despite all of these distractions JJ has remained completely focused on her martial arts and just last month won another world title in the Tae Kwon Do championships in Birmingham. For JJ the buzz has always been about competing and she remains absolutely committed to the sport she loves.

She has won practically everything there is to win in the martial arts world and her next major target must surely be a gold medal at the Olympic Games - although that is likely to be eight years away. Although with the attention she has been receiving from Holywood, the world is literally her oyster. If the 
signals coming out of Holywood are correct we will be seeing JJ in a major blockbuster movie before we see her competing at the Olympics.



JesseJane McParland Martial Arts Champion & Actress

text: I love this movie largely in part for it's well paced and thoughtful story plot that did not choose to rely solely on the typical clobberfest of a superhero storyline. Favorite parts of the film was that the story backdrop was set in 1918 world war 1, which I felt made the story appeal that more interesting. Some of complaints were that I would have loved to have seen more of Steve's secretary "Etta Candy" involved with the comic relief. I also did not buy into the Indian "chief" character in the heart of Europe. I felt that was a little forced upon the audience for possible political correctness reasons but otherwise great movie.

And where did Patty Jenkins get her inspiration for this new version of Wonder Woman? Well, she looked no further than Richard Donner’s 1978 Superman movie (which is also pretty much the inspiration for The CW’s Supergirl TV series which oozes optimism) and explained why:

“Superman was all about you,” said Jenkins. “It was about you watching and realizing what it would feel like to have great powers and do great things. It was full of love and emotion.”

Just because someones different than you does not mean theyre disgusting. And just because wonder woman removes her hair it doesn't mean shes any less powerful than a woman who keeps hers, it just means she chooses to have a different appearance. text: I realized now why I am more excited about THIS movie than I am about the Justice League one. This movie uses LIGHT. it is not afraid of being colorful when it has to be an dark when it serves the story. It is not a constant wish-mash of shadows and rubble.  The main character GLOWS in her full colors like a beacon of hope. You see, "grim and Gritty" is Batman's domain. Every hero has its own soul and I think this one will do a good work capturing Wonder Woman's.  I wish nothing but the best for this one.

According to Greeks, Amazons were Slavic. Gadot is Alpinized Pontid so fits into Slavic phenotype perfectly (and also in Greek). Her grandmother still lives in Poland. She has also German ancestry but her Alpinization from German side is marginal. It comes down to resembling Dasy Ridley from a certain perspective. This leads to a conclusion that she got the role only because of face. Blue lenses and she is perfect Wonder Woman. Strong east Slavic womanhood. But her ectomorphic body of pastoral peoples looks nothing like Wonder Woman. Cinematography and obligatory workouts will fix that. Though Katrina Law woudl be a better Amazon. Gadot is too fragile and introvert.
I think this will be good talkie of its times. That means not memorable, sadly. Surely better than League of Justice although it is also an origin story. Guess why... Pine instead of Affleck.

text: DC has some of the most down-to-Earth and captivating stories in American comics. I pray that this movie will help them find their groove, so to speak. Christopher Nolan did a great job, Zack Snyder did a pretty good job (if he would stop using his hoaky Matrix action effects), and I really did enjoy Batman v Superman. The story was mediocre, but the action sequences were great in my opinion. Suicide Squad was still a pile of garbage, especially after all of the teaser photos that they put out for years before release, unfortunately. They had all the time in the world. I pray that Wonder Woman is a good movie. I have high hopes.

text: The DCEU has stricken out twice. (Technically 3 because man of steel wasn't good by majority, but I won't hold it accountable because it was the start of the universe and I actually thought it was a decent film) I believe that this is the film that can defy whether DC can succeed or fail. But then again there's Justice League that we have to look forward to... and that's really the main factor for this entire DCEU and it's future. But it's really up to whether DC can truly handle that many characters in one movie or not. (something BVS failed to do, not to mention, Justice League is being made by the same person who made BVS, but I'm still having hope.) I really have faith in Wonder Woman and Justice League this year, and I truly hope that my expectations lead into something epic, awesome or at least decent, instead of having to waste my time and money watching a film, just to find out later that they're releasing an "Ultimate Edition or Extended Cut" trying to make us sympathize for it's mistakes in its original cut.

text:
I REALLY want this to be good. I also REALLY hope they don't do that "oh look I'm a strong independent woman but somehow I still need to get rescued all the time" bullshit. Like they try make the feminists happy, and it's like dude, just make A BADASS character not a "some-of-the-time female badass" character. 

I'm so sick of the "Oh women can be strong to" theme. I ALREADY KNOW THAT, NOW FCKING SHOW IT. Chop off a girls head, gut her and have blood spurt everywhere! Not that "oh I have to die in this scene but not epicly, BEAUTIFULLY"

Fcking punch the shit out of each other and die looking horrible like a normal human being.

Lmfao I sound like a sadist or a feminist, but I'm not, I just wanna see something NEW for a change.
Sorry for having an opinion, and people are gonna think this is mainly about gender, it's not.

(Also it may sound like I'm hating but I just have high hopes for the movie, want to make that clear XD)

text:
Here are few Points why I think that in June we will witness a Wonder-FLOP!!
Specially if Rotten "DESTROY" the movie. I already saw 2-3 mistakes on the 
trailers that dont make sense, depending on how they cut the movie.

Ok, Here we go.
1) A Whole Month before will be "Guardian of the Galaxy-2"
so with Logan that make 2 Superhero movies ok so far.

2) 2 and 3 weeks before there are few Big Tittles like: Alien Covenant,Diary of a
wimpy kid and few medium sizes others. Also including 2 from the same Warner,King Arthur  And Everything-Everything,so how many Theaters Warner will get for WW, lets see.
(That will be another factor,theaters count along with the rotten rate %%)

3) Just 1 week before "Pirates of the Caribbean-5", I Personally think that this one will take
all the *Moviegoers MONEY* out there at that time, then how much will be left for WW also 
lets see. 
And Baywatch open same weekend than Pirates, not matter this will be another Flop too!!,
but will take theaters, so less theaters to count!!!

4) W-Woman wont open alone on her first weekend, "Captain Underpants" will be there with her, its an animation movie from Fox and will Open Wide, so distraction for kids and also will take a good amount of all the theaters available for that weekend.

                                                 ***Here Comes the WORST***
5) the weekend after there will be open, I am not sure 2 or 3 movies.
But what I know is that one of those movies is a big tittle, the Reboot of "The Mummy"!!
WITH Tom Cruise, So a BIG,HUGE  name for Gadot to face right the weekend after.

6) 2 weeks after another 3 movies will be release, one of them "Cars-3" another animation 
movie!!!, more distraction for kids.
( ok, on her  weekend premier and on these 2 weeks WW must do WONDERS,talking about $$$, Why?? ok those  are Point 7 & 8)

7) Another 2 Movies!!! (3 weeks after)
One of them "Transformers-The Last Knight" in special this one will need lots, lots of theaters 'cause will be wide, wide released and depending on how good WW is performing 
will tell how bad will be affected in theaters count!!!

8) The WORST!!, Another 4 movies!!! (4 weeks after)
Including """Despicable Me-3", another animation movie that will be out there at the same time and this one will take all the KIDS attention that is for sure!!!!!!
(at that time WW is lucky if she still having 35-40% of the theaters that she open with.)
(That will be pretty bad, really bad  for the MONEY count till the end of her entire run . )

   *** HERE at this Point we already know if is a Flop, for me and a lot others YES!***
                 Because after this line not matter what she does,why?, that is point 9!!

9) Ok, from the fifth week after and beyond came "The Reboot of Spider-Man", "Planet of the Apes-3" and tons of others "BIG TITTLES".

text:
Alright, DC. This is the moment- the turning point. This is your opportunity to make it up to us for Batman Vs. Superman. If you do this movie right, you'll finally have a leg for your DCCU to stand on. You could use it to grow strong; perhaps, one day, even strong enough to challenge Marvel. But if you fail on this movie- if this film people have been waiting for and placing their hopes in for decades bombs like BvS- no one will _ever_ let you forget it. 

I have to say, so far this looks like it might have a chance. It's certainly more _colorful_ than the last few DCCU movies, which is very definitively a good thing; seriously, Zack Snyder, there are other colors besides gray. I'm liking what I'm seeing in this trailer so far, and for once it doesn't feel like either a failed attempt at copying Marvel OR an overly dark and moody social commentary; it feels like an actual superhero movie, with a unique tone and charm and promising plenty of payoff. And that, DC, is why I hope- I _hope_ with every fiber of my being- that THIS is the film on which you *finally* find your spark. Because at this point, Wonder Woman might well be the only character you have left with a fighting chance to save your cinematic universe. 

And so- ignoring my better judgement, ignoring my experience, ignoring what I saw in the last three DCCU films- I am PSYCHED AS HELL for this movie.

text:
****TRIGGER WARNING**** **CONTAINS CRITICISM OF THE DCEU**

I'm not going to get excited over these trailers, no matter how good they look. They've taken us for a ride too many times. I'll see the movie and if I like it, awesome. If not, well... There's another bad DCEU flick. Not a big deal at this point since they've literally ALL been bad. It sucks too, because I've loved these characters my whole life. I don't care about Marvel in the comics so I really just wanted the DC movies to be cool but since they're not I've just had to become detached. We'll probably get a new Batman series at least in a few years and though I don't care about Batman anymore due to his overexposure that might lead to more DC movies that might actually be good so if this one sucks (like it probably will, let's be honest) it's not the end of the world. If it's great I'll be happy but not get my hopes up about Justice League. Either way, I won't get too invested either way at this point.

text: I have to say. Why WWI? It's one of the least justifiable settings for a righteous individual to intervene in. At least in WWII you can state definitively that the Nazi's were evil, what with the whole committing genocide. But WWI was a clusterfuck of imperialism. Every nation involved was complicit in the horror of the great war. So why would Wonder Woman care. Or for that matter side with the Entente over the Germans and their Allies. This makes little sense to me motivation wise. Seems like they're just gonna use this as a colourful backdrop, and substance be damned.

text:
I don't know. The movie looks typical. We watch the hero train, become stronger, go on to win a battle, then we hear of bigger danger to come - sequel. 

DC has had pretty bad luck with the majority of it's films. I'm not sure if it's that they all aim to look darker compared to Marvel's more colorful looking characters. I'm all for it as long as the film is solid (Dark Knight). The majority have not been so good though. I'm neither a Marvel or DC fanboi as I never read either's comics growing up, but from what I've watrched, DC definitely has had the weaker films of the bunch. Maybe they try to make them look too serious and need to lighten up a bit. They are based off comics after all...

text:
I can say straight from - at least half the horses mouth - that the film will surprise people and it is very good.  A friend of mine does sound engineering projects for film studios, it's contract based.  He, among quite a few other groups, did the film scoring for it.  The second by second sound effects work in film.  It's very complicated.  

Wonder Woman is an iconic character born out of tribute for all the women that helped win World War 2.  They built tanks, bombs, munitions, took over the labour glut back home, they did it all.  That's why Wonder Woman is a huge symbol. There is a huge difference between women being empowered and equal and feminism.  The film is a Wonder Woman film, not a political statement.  She's already done that long ago.  Wonder Woman kicks ass, is a Goddess and can take down Superman.  She's a warrior, that's what people want to see.  An apparently the film does just that.  It will probably be WB/ DC"s best movie pound for pound box office wise as well.

Anyways, he worked the rough draft - which essentially the full film.  Missing CGI work and post production work was still being done.  Even though they end up watching the film and an insanely slow snails pace while they add sound, they end up watching the full version after to make sure that timing is right etc.

text:
I have high hopes for this film. I still think Gal Gadot is a bit too skinny for the role, though, which distracts me a little from the character. But maybe it won't matter in the movie; let's see. Anyway, it's a last chance for the DCEU. If this one doen't have at least a coherent story, I won't bother to go to the theater to see any of the future films. Hell, I guess I won't see anything with that not-Superman thing in blue, whatever character that is supposed to be. Perhaps the hope I feel for this Wonder Woman movie comes from it having nothinig to do with the stories and characters that were already butchered. As its own thing, it may work. As a piece of the cinematic universe, I don't care; I'm not sure this universe can be salvaged for me. I would prefer if the whole thing started over.

Bah, sorry for the rant. Concerning this movie, and only that, I'm excited and I hope it works.

text:
Right.  Now we piece together all 3 trailers and see everything sans the final villain showdown. (Please not another blue beam in the sky with a CGI mess in a dark scene attempting to hide outdated CGI and poor editing.)

I was excited for this film, but DC has failed repeatedly to launch a successful CU while playing catch up with Marvel.  I want this film to be outstanding, but, my expectations have been greatly tempered.

Another prequel movie where the results are mostly(if not all) known and thus removing any sense of suspense or weight of consequences.  There are no surprises that she will live and every human(non-enhanced or Deus Ex Machina) will be dead in the timelines once the DCEU stumbles forward with Justice League from this film.  Another group film with 3 characters who receive origin stories AFTER the team up throw down.

I want this to have more staying power than, "Hey, at least the Wonder Woman theme was great!".
author: @MrVilfat

text:
You will train her harder than any amazon before her. 5-times harder. 10-times harder... 

See, normally if you go one on one with another warrior, you got a 50/50 chance of winning. But Diana's a genetic freak and she's not normal! So you got a 25%, AT BEST, at beating her. Then you add Batman to the mix, your chances of winning drastic go down. See the 3-way, at Metropolis, you got a 33 and 1/3 chance of winning, but she got a 66 and 2/3 chance of winning because Batman KNOWS he can't beat her and he's not even gonna try!

So Superman, you take your 33 and 1/3 chance, minus her 25% chance and you got an 8 and 1/3 chance of winning at Metropolis. But then you take her 75% chance of winning, if they were to go one on one, and then add 66 and 2/3 percent, Diana's got 141 and 2/3 chance of winning at Metropolis. See Kal, the numbers don't lie, and they spell disaster for you at Metropolis!

text: God, people are so ungrateful these days. Most comic book fans have been waiting years to see these types of movies and we now live in a time where you can see almost any character you want on the big screen. Instead of being happy, people would rather be edgy and cynical. I have loved both Marvel and DC for my whole life but being invested into these movies suck when everyone thinks they are a fucking movie critic. People need to watch movies for what they are and appreciate them instead of trying to nitpick and destroy a movie because they think it will make them look smart and knowledgeable about movies.

text:
***TRIGGER WARNING*** *CRITICIZING THE DCEU* 

Well, remember how awesome the reports were from BvS initially and look how that turned out. So, yeah, I'm not going to get my hopes up. I'm going to see it, obviously, but at this point WB isn't going to earn my trust until they've put out a few good DC movies. We've been through this three times already and I'm not letting them take me for a ride again. 

NOTE: I'd just like to make clear that I'm not trying to mock people who need trigger warnings. Reading about sexual assault, suicide, self harm, and things like that do legitimately seem to cause psychological damage to some people. The reason for the warning is that it's extremely clear that criticizing these movies in any way (or anything less than effusively praising them) is extremely damaging to certain people's psychological wellbeing so I want to help them out and warn them so they can avoid it and spare their very, very sensitive feelings and extremely fragile psyches. These people clearly need safe spaces and since they're so easily triggered I just want to be a decent person and make sure I don't cause any damage to their already precarious emotional states. Frankly I think they've made it clear that all of us should use trigger warnings when talking about the DCEU with anything less than over-the-top florid praise because it seems that the psychological damage this causes is rather extensive and cuts extremely deep. So if you're going to talk about the DCEU I think you too should keep in mind how easily and severely certain people are triggered by this.

Dear Warner Bros.

text: All you haters, (Marvel Fanboys) are once again, dissing on something that hasn't even come out yet. This movie does look kind of iffy, and having it as a prequel of sorts seems like the only way they could go, due in part of how bad the pacing, continuity, and plot holes BvS put out on the table. Wonder Woman helped, not saved, BvS. So having her movie, debut before Justice League later this year is the smart approach. Where was she when Zod wreaked havoc? When Batman was battling his enemies? When Parallax invaded Coast City? (If they somehow tie Ryan Reynolds GL into this DCEU) Rest assured, they will explain all of those things at the end of the movie, guaranteed. They just need to explain her origin before her appearance in BvS. I'm a little sketchy about the film myself and I'm a DC fan over Marvel but I like both however, you all need to trust the process if we want to see our beloved characters that haven't had screen time yet, besides the MCU which is dragging to end that whole story line. And I also can bet, the Amazonians will be one of the key factors in the Apocalypse story line for Justice League. "I've killed things from other worlds before". Remember that? Just chill until June.

text:
There's something that's in my mind, but it keeps coming in the way sometimes.

So far all the trailers have included few of slow-mo clips in them. I hope WB/DC don't overuse them in the movie, it's not really necessarily, based on the clips, well some are fine. 
You only use slow-mo for something that would make the audience cheers or feel mind-blown for those who loves action or make it feel some kind of dramatic, like for example, at 1:09 we see the amazonians attacking the soldiers/men. It's the first time they discover male genders and the first time we see them on eye-to-eye. It gives us an impression of being mystifying. The point is that we should feel the same thing as how the characters feel, something that's unreal and curious. That's why it's a good placement for slow-mo, it makes it more dramatic and epic at the same time.

I'm hoping that the slow-mos are just used for to make it flow well with the trailer music.

Just an opinion.

text:
As a dedicated comics fan (especially DC Comics), I've been let down by the DC Extended Universe thus far, to say the least. This is a very well put together trailer, and the film looks gorgeous (except for a few obvious green screen shots I hope they improve). If Warner Bros. and DC Films hits one out of the park with Wonder Woman, it could be a game-changer for the studios as well as the genre. I hope it inspires Marvel Studios and 20th Century Fox to trust female-led superhero films are wanted, and can be successful.

I have hope!

author: @seed3803

text:
Why do humans naturally contribute everything to only 2 sides? 
Marvel vs DC
Apple vs Android
Xbox vs Playstation
Consoles vs PC's
Republican vs Democrat
Liberal vs Trump Supporter

It's as if people believe that if you are not for one thing then you MUST be for only one other thing. Or if you are against one thing, then you can only be classified as one thing, and one thing only. For example: Someone could comment on this video, "Doesn't look like it will be a good movie." And they will get attacked with a majority of the comments calling that person a Marvel fanboy. It's as if one could not possibly consider any other variables other than that person MUST only like Marvel. They can't consider the literally hundreds of alternate possibilities to why that person may not think it to be a good movie. Same goes for comments on Marvel videos.

You see this now more than ever in politics. People seem to think that just because a person is critical, or just does not like the President, then they MUST be a "liberal" or a "SJW". Not considering the fact that there are hundreds of groups of types of people, they aren't just "liberals". Same goes for anyone who holds a specific view on in favor of Trump's policies. People think any agreement with him whatsoever, even if it's just one policy will classify that person as a conservative or Republican, or maybe even racist, not considering the hundreds of other variables. 

Perhaps it's because it's easier on our brains not to force ourselves to think outside our own perspective ans calculate other possibilities, so that maybe why we only want things to be 2 sided, so we can have one definitive group of people to blame of misfortunes, rather than have to spell out 10's of different groups. We need to start using our critical thinking a little more. It's for the betterment of the future of society.

text:
The first thing I want to say is that it's always DC fans who call the Marvel fans immature. Just from looking at the comments (without loading more), I've seen 1 person bash DC, and everybody else insulting Marvel fans. How about you all stop acting like there's any reason to "choose a side". Neither company has better characters or better writers. Neither company has better artwork, and the only reason DC's movies haven't been doing well is because they've been making poor decisions for the DCEU. If everybody hated DC just because they're not Marvel, nobody would like their TV shows, or the Dark Knight Trilogy, or the old Superman movies, or their animated series.

Secondly, I might actually go see this now. Might. I'll wait for clips to be released, or feedback from people who aren't professional critics before I decide. That is all.

text:
Look I'm just gonna say this right here, right now. I'm tired of so many people hating this movie and we haven't seen the full finished thing yet. I'm tired of all these fanboys that are like, "F**k this this movie looks like shit. Marvel forever". I'm tired of people bitching about the actors and complaining how the most recent movies in this universe have failed and they believe there's no hope for it left. What is wrong with you people? Why do you insist on spreading hate and negativity all the time when it comes to certain films. WB may have some issues but even I know both them and DC want this universe to succeed and they're trying to please us fans by giving us something we've never seen before. Can't you all just accept the fact and be grateful that we're actually getting a Wonder Woman film? Or are you just angry it's not how you wanted it? Or are you a Marvel fanboy who praises their movies but bashes DC films just because. I may love both franchises equally, but unlike most of you I do not spread negativity all the time whenever there's a new movie. I for one just want a really good movie. And who knows, this may be better than we all expected. I'm tired of the hate. It's unnecessary and it's too much. I'm not a blind fanboy who worships one franchise over the other. I just really want to see this film. 

For all you haters in the comments, you're not helping. You say a movie like this is cancer? Think again.

text: Everything I've seen from this movie looks promising, and I am hoping against hope that this film will give us a better DC than we've been getting. Before you jump down my throat here and call me a blind Marvel fanboy for suggesting that DC's been less than perfect, allow me to state my case. You are, to a certain extent, correct. I do generally enjoy Marvel movies and have no doubts as to their continued success. Aside from Iron Man 2, none of the films in the MCU are outright bad. They all sit pretty close to an enjoyable 3.5/5. Unfortunately, DC movies have lagged behind somewhat. This is deeply dissapointing to me as I am an avid reader of DC comics. Man of Steel is the best so far, and while I don't personally care for it as a Superman film (Donner and Reeve are more my style), it is still perfectly fine as the story of an alien trying to live among us. Plus we're given Michael Shannon's almost perfect performance as General Zod. I give it a solid 3.5/5. Batman V Superman is a largely unsatisfying followup that banks on its complex themes of political unrest, xenophobia, and man's relationship to God, but does nothing more than present these themes, and then loudly congratulate itself on doing so. I give it an even, but unenthusiastic 3/5. Suicide Squad was a mess with awkward pacing, clunky dialogue, cringeworthy humor, dull action, and hollow characters, all topped off with the fact that an infinitely better story could have been told simply by making the Joker the villain. It's so obvious that when I saw the trailers, that's honestly what I thought the movie was going to be! And I was really looking forward to it! It's competently made, though, and if half the stories of studio interference are true, then even that's a miracle that deserves at least a bit of credit. I give it a 2.5/5. Now I'm looking for Wonder Woman to turn it around. I'm not a diehard supporter of the MCU, I haven't been since I was in highschool! I love DC Comics, and I'm not happy about their failures. I desperately want to see their cinematic universe grow to a point where it can rival Marvel's own! I'm going to give WB the benefit of the doubt on Suicide Squad. They were in the process of a tonal shift for the franchise in the wake of BvS, and the film was likely just caught in the crossfire. However, these next two films will be pivotal in securing people's trust. Though Justice League is standing by if this film should fail, right now it falls to Wonder Woman to try and turn the tide. I for one am hoping she can.

author: @arukshan7

text:
I cross my fingers that this is legitimately a good film. I already left the theater 3 times feeling down in the dumps in disappointment after watching all 3 DCEU films. Got so impressed with the trailers that were released prior to those 3 films, that I was genuinely hyped, but then I had the opposite reaction after watching the actually films. I feel this scenario won't be any different. I just hope I'm not wrong.

I can't fathom that this is following the impressive (in my personal opinion) Dark Knight Trilogy (BB was pretty good, TDK was amazing, and TDKR a little bit of both, though regardless I couldn't take Christian Bale seriously!). But I'm willing to give this film a chance in order to support Wonder Woman. And this is coming from a person who genuinely grew up loving both DC and Marvel back in the 90s. 

But so help me, if this film ends up being another stinker, I'm so not supporting this film series anymore! Will stick with the DC TV Universe, animated films and animated shows (especially the upcoming Young Justice season 3).

text: Ok, this is pissing me off. Can't we both accept that DC and Marvel are capable of making good movies? Each company has their strength and weaknesses. Marvel fails to have many memorable soundtracks and villains, DC can lack in the character development department. They're both good companies and they're both just trying to please their fans. Just because you don't like a DC movie does not mean you have to make fun of those who do enjoy watching that film. And vice versa, i've seen way too many DC fans trashtalking and harassing Marvel fans. Just accept that they both make good movies and if you don't like their movies then they weren't made for you.

text:
I dont understand how people are hyped for this. They're in for huge dissapointment, *again*, since this is DC we're talking about.

Gal's skinny ass looks nothing like Wonder Woman. She doesnt sound like her either. And no one can say I'm unfairly doubting her, or some shit, because we've seen her in BvS. She didnt impress me then, and hasnt done so now. And before anyone says its unfair to judge from a trailer, thats not the case if trailers spoil *alot*. The only thing these WW trailers havent spoiled, is the villain, *yet*.

I swear, most people that are looking forward to this movie, are blinded by her theme song. Granted, its good, but I can think of far better theme songs, like Superman or Batman's. Speaking of which, WW's theme song wouldnt have as much attention, if *all* the music in BvS had emotion.
author: @Walt_Xander94

text: With DC sending Supergirl, Batgirl and Wonder Woman action figures into stores for the younger demographic, it is excellent for them to continue empowering women and girls to be strong and self reliant. Marvel has continued to reinvent the Spider-Man character over and over again. Spider-Woman would be an amazing character to see on the big screen, if you've ever seen the Michael Turner art for Spider-Woman, it speaks for itself, as does all of his renderings for Wonder Woman, really beautiful artwork. After seeing the Fast and Furious movies, it may have been the obvious choice to select the muscular female actors over Gal Gadot to play Wonder Woman, but regardless of how the movie succeeds overall, the casting was handled perfectly.

text:
Issues: 1. Didn't explain her positioning in her community. 
             2. Doesn't explain why they're in London or why/what they're fighting there.
             3. Doesn't explain who the man is or even a name. 
             4. Seems like a counterfeit Man of Steel -- shunned as a child, over-feared as an adult. 
             5. Lacks comedy. 

Positives: Gal Gadot is hot. Only reason people will see the movie. 

I understand it's just a trailer, and even being generous, this one was 4/10 compared to your standard superhero movies. And I'm pretty sure the audience of MoS and BvS have expressed their tiredness of this grayscale imagery. This trailer explained nothing. What is she fighting? Where is the danger?

text:
Edit: Seems like the blind fanboys got here quick. I love DC and I love Marvel. Because I love and understand these characters, I feel like the DCEU hasn't delivered. In some aspects yes (finally got Batman's physicality and combat right). That's MY opinion, which goes unsaid. 

Another thing. You don't HAVE to love something just because it has characters you love. If you genuinely like the DCEU movies and see no flaws in writing, character portrayals, lack of development/motivation, rushing through stories, or forced set ups, then that's ok. But because I'm a huge fan, I expect high quality films for 2 reasons:

The first being that these characters deserve the best movie possible (Wolverine just got the movie he's deserved since the beginning). The second reason is that we, the fans, deserve better. Don't eat everything they feed you.

text: I've definitely loved and enjoyed Man Of Steel, Batman v Superman and Suicide Squad, I don't need any explanations of why for you they were bad because I don't care what you all think, I'm gonna watch this movie and love it all the way through, because the fact that we will see Wonder Woman for the first time ever in a solo film is amazing and exciting, everyone should stop sticking into what they hate and love what they love, because if one thing is certain is that DC is getting more stronger and great than ever, and the film will rock it out of the park, that's for sure and fine by me; if you don't wanna see this movie, then don't watch it and that's it, but don't loose excitement and enthusiasm over something just because it didn't work out the first time, I'm excited for Wonder Woman, Justice League and Aquaman, I'm excited for all the upcoming films that are going to come out, there are so many great things going so well and big here in geek culture that instead of complaining all the time like whiney idiots, we should be excited about the things that are about to happen and feeling very hyped about it, it's only a word of advice, if you don't wanna take it, is fine, just enjoy the film and that's it, don't criticize it on a critical level, don't analyse it on editing, sound and all that crap, just see the damn film and whether you like it or not, your life goes on; I'm excited about Wonder Woman and what I believe in the passion of Patty Jenkins and Gal Gadot is that they were tasked in creating something big and important, and that's what WW is all about, I'm superexcited to see the film and I don't care what you think

text:
I'm quite positive I'm going to love this movie, but the trailer doesn't live up to it.

It's a paradox, in my opinion: the movie with the best chance to be a absolute success (and not a mild one with confrontations between casuals, hardcore fans and cinephiles like the other three DCEU movies) has the most "mediocre" trailer, when we compare it to so many others:

> Suicide Squad delivered three solid trailers
> Batman v Superman, despite the HUGE spoilers, also had three incredible works.
> Man of Steel had masterpieces of trailers.
> Even many recents trailers (Logan, Guardians of the Galaxy) have established the movies in such a perfect way, and they are all competing in the same genre!

What i'm trying to say here is that, although I think the movie can be really good, the trailers are not AS GOOD as they need to be in order to set up the correct expectation. It's not only about visual spectacle, astonishing music and showing off awesome scenes: it's about the vibes, the spirit of the character and the feeling that we are truly getting the movie that Wonder Woman deserves and an incredible experience.

And sorry, I did not feel anything while watching this. I wish I had.

text: The problem with DC films is the constant preaching and zero subtle moments. Yes, Dark Knight Trilogy was very preachy at times but there were some really genuine moments that you actually believe it could be real. There were also moments in Nolan's trilogy where you were like "WOW!!". And a lot of times Nolan concentrated more on practical effects and more on the character development and story. Unfortunately, Wonderwoman is going to be another DC stinker. Hopefully, I am proven wrong because I really like the character but Warner Bros have a knack for butchering awesome DC characters. Warner Bros should stop interfering and the directors should focus more on the characters, story, dialogue, subtle moments, and practical effects.

text: I feel like this needs to be said anytime a trailer like this comes out: YOU CAN STILL ENJOY MARVEL AND DC DESPITE WHATEVER FLAWS EACH FRANCHISE HAS. You can be excited for the colorful, funny, and just plain weirdness of Guardians of the Galaxy and still be excited for the somber and inspiring Wonder Woman. I personally have my gripes with both franchises but I still would never say I outright hate them. We are comic book fans and there has never been a time like now where we should all be excited for the movies that are coming out. Now is not the time where fans should be at each other's throats but instead should be celebrating the sheer awesomeness of the fact that this year we will be getting a sequel to Guardians of the Galaxy, an actual Wonder Woman movie, what appears to be the most comic accurate version of Spider-Man ever put to film, what looks to be the best Thor movie and the closest we will ever get to a Planet Hulk movie, and last but not least we are getting the freaking JUSTICE LEAGUE this year! Talk about comic book movie HYPE!!!!!!
